Now I have the full picture. Here is the detailed Phase 8 plan:

Phase 8: Advanced Features & Differentiators — Full Plan
Honest complexity assessment first
Before sequencing, let me grade each feature:

#	Feature	Backend complexity	Value
8.6	Data Health Monitor	EASY — data already exists	High
8.2	Goal tracking	EASY — goals table + progress query	High
8.9	Scorecard system	EASY — comparison logic already in MetricsService	High
8.1	Alerts & monitoring	MEDIUM — new table + post-sync hook	High
8.10	Client communication hub	MEDIUM — notes table + CRUD	Medium
8.11	Template marketplace	MEDIUM — flag on existing models	Medium
8.8	ROI forecasting	MEDIUM — linear regression on metric_values	High
8.3	KPI engine	MEDIUM-HARD — formula evaluation, correctness matters	High
8.7	Data export	MEDIUM — BullMQ job + exceljs	Medium
8.4	AI report explanation	MEDIUM — one Claude API call	High
8.5	AI assistant	HARD — streaming, conversation history, prompt engineering	Very High
8.12	Billing (Stripe)	HARD — webhooks, plan enforcement, Stripe integration	Critical
Recommended implementation order
Build easy → medium → hard, with related features grouped:

8.6 — Data Health Monitor (backend: 1 endpoint)
What it is: Surface IntegrationConnection status as a structured health response. The data already exists — this is just a query + formatting layer.

DB: No new tables.

New endpoint:

GET /campaigns/:id/health — returns per-integration: status, lastSyncAt, lastErrorAt, lastErrorMessage, platform. Groups as healthy[], expired[], error[], disconnected[].
One addition: GET /agencies/health — aggregate across all campaigns for the agency dashboard summary card.

8.2 — Goal Tracking
DB: goals table — id, tenant_id, campaign_id, platform, metric_key, target_value, period_type (WEEKLY/MONTHLY/QUARTERLY), period_start, period_end, label, created_by_id, soft delete

Endpoints:

POST /campaigns/:id/goals — create goal
GET /campaigns/:id/goals — list goals
GET /campaigns/:id/goals/:goalId/progress — returns { target, actual, percentage, status: ON_TRACK | AT_RISK | BEHIND }
actual = MetricsService.getMetricSummary() for the goal period
ON_TRACK = actual ≥ 70% of target; AT_RISK = 40–70%; BEHIND = <40%
PATCH /campaigns/:id/goals/:goalId
DELETE /campaigns/:id/goals/:goalId
8.9 — Scorecard System
What it is: Period-over-period comparison for any metric. Already partially implemented in MetricsService (comparison period support exists in the batch widget endpoint). This is a clean standalone endpoint.

DB: No new tables.

New endpoint:

GET /campaigns/:id/scorecard?platform=GA4&from=...&to=...&metricKeys=sessions,clicks — returns per metric: { current, previous, changePercent, status: GOOD | WARNING | BAD }. Uses existing getMetricSummary twice (current + prior period).
Status logic: GOOD = positive change or within 5%; WARNING = -5% to -15%; BAD = worse than -15%. Configurable thresholds later.

8.1 — Alerts & Monitoring
DB:

alert_rules — id, tenant_id, campaign_id, platform, metric_key, operator (ABOVE/BELOW), threshold, period_days, notification_emails[], is_active, last_triggered_at
alert_events — id, tenant_id, rule_id, actual_value, threshold, triggered_at (history)
How it works:

After every sync job completes (IntegrationSyncProcessor), fire a POST_SYNC_CHECK BullMQ job
AlertCheckProcessor queries active rules for the synced campaign+platform, evaluates each rule using MetricsService.getMetricSummary, compares against threshold
If triggered and not triggered in last cooldown_hours (default 24h): send email, create alert_event
Endpoints:

POST /campaigns/:id/alert-rules — create rule
GET /campaigns/:id/alert-rules — list
PATCH /campaigns/:id/alert-rules/:id — update/toggle
DELETE /campaigns/:id/alert-rules/:id
GET /campaigns/:id/alert-events — history (last 100)
8.10 — Client Communication Hub
DB: campaign_notes — id, tenant_id, campaign_id, author_id, body (text, max 5000), is_pinned, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at

Endpoints:

POST /campaigns/:id/notes
GET /campaigns/:id/notes — paginated, pinned first
PATCH /campaigns/:id/notes/:noteId
DELETE /campaigns/:id/notes/:noteId (soft delete, own notes only for STAFF; any for ADMIN)
Access: AGENCY_STAFF+, CLIENT_USER (read + create own). AGENCY_ADMIN can delete any.

8.11 — Template Marketplace
DB: Add to existing tables:

dashboards: add is_template BOOL DEFAULT FALSE, template_name VARCHAR(255), template_description TEXT
reports: add is_template BOOL DEFAULT FALSE, template_name VARCHAR(255)
Endpoints:

POST /campaigns/:id/dashboards/:id/publish-template — marks dashboard as template, copies it to system templates
GET /templates/dashboards — public browseable list (@Public)
POST /campaigns/:id/dashboards/from-template/:templateId — clone template into campaign
Same pattern for report templates
8.8 — ROI Forecasting
What it is: Linear regression on last 60–90 days of metric_values → project N days forward.

DB: No new tables.

New endpoint:

GET /campaigns/:id/forecast?platform=GA4&metricKey=sessions&days=30 — returns { historical: [...], projected: [...] }. projected contains dates from today+1 to today+days with predicted values. Uses least-squares linear regression on metric_values.
Implementation: Pure math in ForecastService — no ML library needed. y = mx + b fit over historical points, project forward.

8.3 — KPI Engine (custom calculated metrics)
DB: kpi_definitions — id, tenant_id, campaign_id?, label, formula (string e.g. "(revenue - cost) / cost * 100"), input_metric_keys[], platform, created_by_id

How it works:

KpiEngineService.evaluate(definition, from, to) — fetches input_metric_keys via MetricsService.getMetricSummary, substitutes values into formula using a safe expression evaluator (mathjs or manual AST)
Dashboard widgets can reference a kpi_definition_id instead of raw metricKeys
Reports same
Security: Formula is evaluated in a sandboxed parser — no eval(). Use mathjs expression parser which is safe by design.

8.7 — Data Export
What it is: Export any metric query to Excel (.xlsx). Google Sheets API is complex OAuth flow — ship Excel first.

New package: exceljs

DB: export_jobs — id, tenant_id, status, download_url, error_msg, created_at

Endpoints:

POST /campaigns/:id/exports — body: { platform, metricKeys, from, to, format: "xlsx" } — queues BullMQ job, returns { jobId }
GET /campaigns/:id/exports/:jobId — returns status + download URL when ready
BullMQ processor: Fetches metrics → builds xlsx with exceljs → uploads to MinIO → emails download link → updates job status.

8.4 — AI Report Explanation
What it is: After a report is generated, send metrics to Claude API and get a plain-English summary.

New env: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

New endpoint:

POST /campaigns/:id/reports/:id/explain — fetches report sections, calls Claude claude-haiku-4-5 (fast + cheap for summaries), returns { explanation: string }. Stores on report as aiExplanation TEXT.
DB: Add ai_explanation TEXT to reports table.

Prompt pattern: Structured context with actual metric values + period, ask for 2–3 paragraph plain-English analysis. Max 1000 tokens output.

8.5 — AI Assistant (Campaign Q&A)
What it is: Chat interface over campaign data. Most complex feature in Phase 8.

New endpoint:

POST /campaigns/:id/ai/query — body: { question: string, sessionId?: string } — streaming response (text/event-stream)
How it works:

Parse question to identify intent (which platform, metric, time range)
Fetch relevant metrics from MetricsService
Build prompt: system context (campaign name, date range, actual numbers) + user question
Stream Claude response back via SSE
Store conversation in Redis (session-based, 1h TTL)
DB: No persistent storage for chat (Redis only) — can add ai_conversations table later.

8.12 — Billing (Stripe)
The most infrastructure-heavy feature. Plan in full:

DB:

subscriptions — id, tenant_id, stripe_subscription_id, stripe_customer_id, plan (FREELANCER/AGENCY/AGENCY_PRO), status, current_period_end, cancel_at_period_end
plan_limits — hardcoded in config: max clients, max integrations, max seats per plan
Endpoints:

POST /billing/checkout — creates Stripe Checkout session, returns URL
POST /billing/portal — creates Stripe Customer Portal session, returns URL
GET /billing/subscription — current plan + usage
POST /billing/webhook — Stripe webhook handler (subscription.created/updated/deleted, invoice.paid/failed)
Plan enforcement: UsageLimitGuard — checked before creating clients, campaigns, integrations. Reads plan from subscription, compares against current count.

New env: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET

Implementation sequence
Given complexity and dependencies:


8.6 (health monitor) — 1 day, no deps
8.2 (goals)          — 1 day, no deps
8.9 (scorecard)      — 0.5 day, uses existing metrics
8.10 (notes)         — 1 day, no deps
8.1 (alerts)         — 2 days, depends on sync flow
8.11 (templates)     — 1 day, depends on dashboards + reports
8.8 (forecasting)    — 1 day, depends on metrics
8.3 (KPI engine)     — 2 days, depends on metrics
8.7 (export)         — 1.5 days, depends on metrics + storage
8.4 (AI explain)     — 1 day, depends on reports + Claude API
8.5 (AI assistant)   — 3 days, depends on metrics + Claude streaming
8.12 (billing)       — 3 days, independent but last (enforcement touches all modules)
This is the complete Phase 8 plan — all 12 features, all backend only. Shall I start implementing from 8.6 → 8.2 → 8.9 in one shot, or do you want to go feature by feature?

import { Injectable, NotFoundException } from '@nestjs/common';
import { UserRole, Prisma, IntegrationPlatform, ConnectionStatus } from '@prisma/client';
import { PrismaService } from '../../database/prisma.service';
import { EncryptionService } from '../../common/encryption/encryption.service';
import { AuthenticatedUser } from '../auth/strategies/jwt.strategy';
import { UpsertIntegrationDto } from './dto/upsert-integration.dto';

@Injectable()
export class IntegrationsService {
  constructor(
    private readonly prisma: PrismaService,
    private readonly encryption: EncryptionService,
  ) {}

  // ─── Upsert ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  // Creates the connection if it does not exist; updates it if it does.
  //
  // Token safety rule: existing encrypted tokens are NEVER overwritten unless
  // new plaintext tokens are explicitly provided in the DTO.
  // This prevents accidental token loss during status-only updates.

  async upsert(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
    dto: UpsertIntegrationDto,
  ) {
    await this.assertCampaignAccess(user, clientId, campaignId);

    // Encrypt new tokens only if provided
    const accessTokenEnc = dto.accessToken
      ? this.encryption.encrypt(dto.accessToken)
      : undefined;
    const refreshTokenEnc = dto.refreshToken
      ? this.encryption.encrypt(dto.refreshToken)
      : undefined;

    // Determine status: if new tokens are provided, mark CONNECTED; otherwise keep existing
    const statusOnCreate: ConnectionStatus = dto.accessToken
      ? ConnectionStatus.CONNECTED
      : ConnectionStatus.DISCONNECTED;

    const connection = await this.prisma.integrationConnection.upsert({
      where: {
        campaignId_platform: { campaignId, platform: dto.platform },
      },

      create: {
        tenantId: user.tenantId,
        campaignId,
        platform: dto.platform,
        status: statusOnCreate,
        ...(accessTokenEnc !== undefined && { accessTokenEnc }),
        ...(refreshTokenEnc !== undefined && { refreshTokenEnc }),
        ...(dto.tokenExpiresAt && { tokenExpiresAt: new Date(dto.tokenExpiresAt) }),
        ...(dto.refreshTokenExpiresAt && { refreshTokenExpiresAt: new Date(dto.refreshTokenExpiresAt) }),
        ...(dto.scopes !== undefined && { scopes: dto.scopes }),
        ...(dto.externalAccountId !== undefined && { externalAccountId: dto.externalAccountId }),
        ...(dto.platformAccountType !== undefined && { platformAccountType: dto.platformAccountType }),
      },

      update: {
        // Only overwrite tokens if new ones are explicitly provided
        ...(accessTokenEnc !== undefined && { accessTokenEnc }),
        ...(refreshTokenEnc !== undefined && { refreshTokenEnc }),
        // If new access token provided, mark CONNECTED and clear any previous error
        ...(dto.accessToken && {
          status: ConnectionStatus.CONNECTED,
          lastErrorAt: null,
          lastErrorMessage: null,
        }),
        ...(dto.tokenExpiresAt && { tokenExpiresAt: new Date(dto.tokenExpiresAt) }),
        ...(dto.refreshTokenExpiresAt && { refreshTokenExpiresAt: new Date(dto.refreshTokenExpiresAt) }),
        ...(dto.scopes !== undefined && { scopes: dto.scopes }),
        ...(dto.externalAccountId !== undefined && { externalAccountId: dto.externalAccountId }),
        ...(dto.platformAccountType !== undefined && { platformAccountType: dto.platformAccountType }),
      },

      select: this.publicSelect(),
    });

import {
  Injectable,
  NotFoundException,
} from '@nestjs/common';
import { UserRole, Prisma } from '@prisma/client';
import { PrismaService } from '../../database/prisma.service';
import { AuthenticatedUser } from '../auth/strategies/jwt.strategy';
import { CreateCampaignDto } from './dto/create-campaign.dto';
import { UpdateCampaignDto } from './dto/update-campaign.dto';
import { CampaignQueryDto } from './dto/campaign-query.dto';
import { DashboardsService } from '../dashboards/dashboards.service';

@Injectable()
export class CampaignsService {

    private readonly prisma: PrismaService,
    private readonly dashboardsService: DashboardsService,
  ) {}

    // ADMIN/OWNER can create on any tenant client — no assignment check needed.
    const client = await this.prisma.client.findFirst({
      where: { id: clientId, tenantId: user.tenantId, deletedAt: null },

-- CreateEnum
CREATE TYPE "goal_period_type" AS ENUM ('WEEKLY', 'MONTHLY', 'QUARTERLY');

-- CreateEnum
CREATE TYPE "goal_status" AS ENUM ('ON_TRACK', 'AT_RISK', 'BEHIND', 'ACHIEVED');

-- CreateTable
CREATE TABLE "goals" (
    "id" UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    "tenant_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "campaign_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "name" VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    "platform" "integration_platform" NOT NULL,
    "metric_key" VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    "target_value" DECIMAL(20,6) NOT NULL,
    "period_type" "goal_period_type" NOT NULL DEFAULT 'MONTHLY',
    "period_start" DATE NOT NULL,
    "period_end" DATE NOT NULL,
    "created_by_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "created_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "updated_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "deleted_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6),

    CONSTRAINT "goals_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);

-- CreateTable
CREATE TABLE "campaign_notes" (
    "id" UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    "tenant_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "campaign_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "author_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "body" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "is_pinned" BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT false,
    "created_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "updated_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "deleted_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6),

    CONSTRAINT "campaign_notes_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);

-- CreateIndex
CREATE INDEX "idx_goals_campaign" ON "goals"("tenant_id", "campaign_id", "deleted_at");

-- CreateIndex
CREATE INDEX "idx_goals_period" ON "goals"("tenant_id", "period_start", "period_end");

-- CreateIndex
CREATE INDEX "idx_campaign_notes_campaign" ON "campaign_notes"("tenant_id", "campaign_id", "deleted_at");

-- CreateIndex
CREATE INDEX "idx_campaign_notes_pinned" ON "campaign_notes"("tenant_id", "campaign_id", "is_pinned") WHERE "deleted_at" IS NULL;

-- AddForeignKey
ALTER TABLE "goals" ADD CONSTRAINT "goals_campaign_id_fkey" FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES "campaigns"("id") ON DELETE NO ACTION ON UPDATE NO ACTION;

-- AddForeignKey
ALTER TABLE "goals" ADD CONSTRAINT "goals_tenant_id_fkey" FOREIGN KEY ("tenant_id") REFERENCES "agencies"("id") ON DELETE NO ACTION ON UPDATE NO ACTION;

-- AddForeignKey
ALTER TABLE "campaign_notes" ADD CONSTRAINT "campaign_notes_campaign_id_fkey" FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES "campaigns"("id") ON DELETE CASCADE ON UPDATE NO ACTION;

-- AddForeignKey
ALTER TABLE "campaign_notes" ADD CONSTRAINT "campaign_notes_tenant_id_fkey" FOREIGN KEY ("tenant_id") REFERENCES "agencies"("id") ON DELETE NO ACTION ON UPDATE NO ACTION;

-- Enable RLS
ALTER TABLE "goals" ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
ALTER TABLE "campaign_notes" ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;

-- RLS Policies
CREATE POLICY "tenant_isolation" ON "goals"
  USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant', true)::uuid);

CREATE POLICY "tenant_isolation" ON "campaign_notes"
  USING (tenant_id = current_setting('app.current_tenant', true)::uuid);

Write health.service.ts

import { Injectable, NotFoundException } from '@nestjs/common';
import { ConnectionStatus, UserRole } from '@prisma/client';
import { PrismaService } from '../../database/prisma.service';
import { AuthenticatedUser } from '../auth/strategies/jwt.strategy';

export interface IntegrationHealthItem {
  platform: string;
  status: ConnectionStatus;
  lastSyncAt: Date | null;
  lastErrorAt: Date | null;
  lastErrorMessage: string | null;
  externalAccountId: string | null;
}

export interface CampaignHealthResponse {
  campaignId: string;
  campaignName: string;
  integrations: IntegrationHealthItem[];
  summary: {
    total: number;
    connected: number;
    expired: number;
    error: number;
    disconnected: number;
  };
}

export interface AgencyHealthResponse {
  summary: {
    totalCampaigns: number;
    totalIntegrations: number;
    connected: number;
    expired: number;
    error: number;
    disconnected: number;
  };
  campaigns: Array<{
    campaignId: string;
    campaignName: string;
    clientName: string;
    connectedCount: number;
    expiredCount: number;
    errorCount: number;
  }>;
}

const CLIENT_VISIBLE_ROLES = new Set([UserRole.CLIENT_USER]);

@Injectable()
export class HealthService {
  constructor(private readonly prisma: PrismaService) {}

  async getCampaignHealth(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
  ): Promise<CampaignHealthResponse> {
    const campaign = await this.prisma.campaign.findFirst({
      where: this.campaignWhere(user, clientId, campaignId),
      select: { id: true, name: true },
    });

    if (!campaign) throw new NotFoundException('Campaign not found.');

    const connections = await this.prisma.integrationConnection.findMany({
      where: { tenantId: user.tenantId, campaignId },
      select: {
        platform: true,
        status: true,
        lastSyncAt: true,
        lastErrorAt: true,
        lastErrorMessage: true,
        externalAccountId: true,
      },
      orderBy: { platform: 'asc' },
    });

    const summary = { total: 0, connected: 0, expired: 0, error: 0, disconnected: 0 };
    for (const c of connections) {
      summary.total++;
      switch (c.status) {
        case ConnectionStatus.CONNECTED: summary.connected++; break;
        case ConnectionStatus.EXPIRED: summary.expired++; break;
        case ConnectionStatus.ERROR: summary.error++; break;
        case ConnectionStatus.DISCONNECTED: summary.disconnected++; break;
      }
    }

    return {
      campaignId: campaign.id,
      campaignName: campaign.name,
      integrations: connections,
      summary,
    };
  }

  async getAgencyHealth(user: AuthenticatedUser): Promise<AgencyHealthResponse> {
    // CLIENT_USER sees only their assigned campaigns; staff/admin see all
    const isClient = CLIENT_VISIBLE_ROLES.has(user.role as UserRole);

    const connections = await this.prisma.integrationConnection.findMany({
      where: {
        tenantId: user.tenantId,
        ...(isClient && {
          campaign: {
            client: {
              clientUserAssignments: { some: { userId: user.id } },
            },
          },
        }),
      },
      select: {
        status: true,
        campaignId: true,
        campaign: {
          select: {
            id: true,
            name: true,
            client: { select: { name: true } },
            deletedAt: true,
          },
        },
      },
    });

    // Exclude soft-deleted campaigns
    const live = connections.filter(c => !c.campaign.deletedAt);

    // Build per-campaign map
    const campaignMap = new Map<string, {
      campaignId: string;
      campaignName: string;
      clientName: string;
      connectedCount: number;
      expiredCount: number;
      errorCount: number;
    }>();

    const agencySummary = { totalIntegrations: 0, connected: 0, expired: 0, error: 0, disconnected: 0 };

    for (const c of live) {
      agencySummary.totalIntegrations++;
      switch (c.status) {
        case ConnectionStatus.CONNECTED: agencySummary.connected++; break;
        case ConnectionStatus.EXPIRED: agencySummary.expired++; break;
        case ConnectionStatus.ERROR: agencySummary.error++; break;
        case ConnectionStatus.DISCONNECTED: agencySummary.disconnected++; break;
      }

      if (!campaignMap.has(c.campaignId)) {
        campaignMap.set(c.campaignId, {
          campaignId: c.campaignId,
          campaignName: c.campaign.name,
          clientName: c.campaign.client.name,
          connectedCount: 0,
          expiredCount: 0,
          errorCount: 0,
        });
      }
      const row = campaignMap.get(c.campaignId)!;
      if (c.status === ConnectionStatus.CONNECTED) row.connectedCount++;
      else if (c.status === ConnectionStatus.EXPIRED) row.expiredCount++;
      else if (c.status === ConnectionStatus.ERROR) row.errorCount++;
    }

    return {
      summary: {
        totalCampaigns: campaignMap.size,
        ...agencySummary,
      },
      campaigns: Array.from(campaignMap.values()),
    };
  }

  private campaignWhere(user: AuthenticatedUser, clientId: string, campaignId: string) {
    const isClient = CLIENT_VISIBLE_ROLES.has(user.role as UserRole);
    return {
      id: campaignId,
      clientId,
      tenantId: user.tenantId,
      deletedAt: null,
      ...(isClient && {
        client: {
          clientUserAssignments: { some: { userId: user.id } },
        },
      }),
    };
  }
}
Write health.controller.ts

import { Controller, Get, Param } from '@nestjs/common';
import { ApiTags, ApiBearerAuth, ApiOperation } from '@nestjs/swagger';
import { HealthService } from './health.service';
import { CurrentUser } from '../../common/decorators/current-user.decorator';
import { AuthenticatedUser } from '../auth/strategies/jwt.strategy';

@ApiTags('Health')
@ApiBearerAuth()
@Controller()
export class HealthController {
  constructor(private readonly healthService: HealthService) {}

  @Get('clients/:clientId/campaigns/:campaignId/health')
  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Get integration health for a single campaign' })
  getCampaignHealth(
    @CurrentUser() user: AuthenticatedUser,
    @Param('clientId') clientId: string,
    @Param('campaignId') campaignId: string,
  ) {
    return this.healthService.getCampaignHealth(user, clientId, campaignId);
  }

  @Get('agencies/health')
  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Get integration health summary across all campaigns' })
  getAgencyHealth(@CurrentUser() user: AuthenticatedUser) {
    return this.healthService.getAgencyHealth(user);
  }
}

export class HealthModule {}

import {
  IsString, IsNotEmpty, IsEnum, IsDateString,
  IsNumber, Min, MaxLength,
} from 'class-validator';
import { ApiProperty } from '@nestjs/swagger';
import { IntegrationPlatform, GoalPeriodType } from '@prisma/client';

export class CreateGoalDto {
  @ApiProperty({ example: 'Reach 10k sessions' })
  @IsString()
  @IsNotEmpty()
  @MaxLength(255)
  name: string;

  @ApiProperty({ enum: IntegrationPlatform })
  @IsEnum(IntegrationPlatform)
  platform: IntegrationPlatform;

  @ApiProperty({ example: 'sessions' })
  @IsString()
  @IsNotEmpty()
  @MaxLength(100)
  metricKey: string;

  @ApiProperty({ example: 10000 })
  @IsNumber()
  @Min(0)
  targetValue: number;

  @ApiProperty({ enum: GoalPeriodType, default: GoalPeriodType.MONTHLY })
  @IsEnum(GoalPeriodType)
  periodType: GoalPeriodType;

  @ApiProperty({ example: '2026-04-01' })
  @IsDateString()
  periodStart: string;

  @ApiProperty({ example: '2026-04-30' })
  @IsDateString()
  periodEnd: string;
}

import { CreateGoalDto } from './create-goal.dto';

export class UpdateGoalDto extends PartialType(CreateGoalDto) {}
Write goals.service.ts
223 lines
import {
  Injectable,
  NotFoundException,
  ForbiddenException,
  BadRequestException,
} from '@nestjs/common';
import { UserRole, IntegrationPlatform, GoalPeriodType } from '@prisma/client';
import { PrismaService } from '../../database/prisma.service';
import { MetricsService } from '../metrics/metrics.service';
import { MetricAggregate } from '../metrics/dto/query-metrics.dto';
import { AuthenticatedUser } from '../auth/strategies/jwt.strategy';
import { CreateGoalDto } from './dto/create-goal.dto';
import { UpdateGoalDto } from './dto/update-goal.dto';

const ADMIN_ROLES: UserRole[] = [UserRole.AGENCY_OWNER, UserRole.AGENCY_ADMIN];

// Progress thresholds (percentage of target achieved)
const ON_TRACK_THRESHOLD = 0.7;  // ≥ 70%
const AT_RISK_THRESHOLD = 0.4;   // 40–70%
// < 40% = BEHIND; ≥ 100% = ACHIEVED

@Injectable()
export class GoalsService {
  constructor(
    private readonly prisma: PrismaService,
    private readonly metrics: MetricsService,
  ) {}

  // ─── CRUD ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  async create(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
    dto: CreateGoalDto,
  ) {
    this.assertAdmin(user);
    await this.assertCampaignAccess(user, clientId, campaignId);
    this.assertDateRange(dto.periodStart, dto.periodEnd);

    return this.prisma.goal.create({
      data: {
        tenantId: user.tenantId,
        campaignId,
        name: dto.name,
        platform: dto.platform,
        metricKey: dto.metricKey,
        targetValue: dto.targetValue,
        periodType: dto.periodType,
        periodStart: new Date(dto.periodStart),
        periodEnd: new Date(dto.periodEnd),
        createdById: user.id,
      },
    });
  }

  async list(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
  ) {
    await this.assertCampaignAccess(user, clientId, campaignId);

    return this.prisma.goal.findMany({
      where: { tenantId: user.tenantId, campaignId, deletedAt: null },
      orderBy: [{ periodStart: 'desc' }, { createdAt: 'desc' }],
    });
  }

  async update(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
    goalId: string,
    dto: UpdateGoalDto,
  ) {
    this.assertAdmin(user);
    await this.assertCampaignAccess(user, clientId, campaignId);

    const goal = await this.findGoal(user.tenantId, campaignId, goalId);

    const startDate = dto.periodStart ?? goal.periodStart.toISOString().slice(0, 10);
    const endDate = dto.periodEnd ?? goal.periodEnd.toISOString().slice(0, 10);
    if (dto.periodStart || dto.periodEnd) {
      this.assertDateRange(startDate, endDate);
    }

    return this.prisma.goal.update({
      where: { id: goalId },
      data: {
        ...(dto.name !== undefined && { name: dto.name }),
        ...(dto.platform !== undefined && { platform: dto.platform }),
        ...(dto.metricKey !== undefined && { metricKey: dto.metricKey }),
        ...(dto.targetValue !== undefined && { targetValue: dto.targetValue }),
        ...(dto.periodType !== undefined && { periodType: dto.periodType }),
        ...(dto.periodStart !== undefined && { periodStart: new Date(dto.periodStart) }),
        ...(dto.periodEnd !== undefined && { periodEnd: new Date(dto.periodEnd) }),
      },
    });
  }

  async remove(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
    goalId: string,
  ) {
    this.assertAdmin(user);
    await this.assertCampaignAccess(user, clientId, campaignId);
    await this.findGoal(user.tenantId, campaignId, goalId);

    await this.prisma.goal.update({
      where: { id: goalId },
      data: { deletedAt: new Date() },
    });
  }

  // ─── Progress ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  async getProgress(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
    goalId: string,
  ) {
    await this.assertCampaignAccess(user, clientId, campaignId);
    const goal = await this.findGoal(user.tenantId, campaignId, goalId);

    const from = goal.periodStart.toISOString().slice(0, 10);
    const to = goal.periodEnd.toISOString().slice(0, 10);

    const summary = await this.metrics.getMetricSummary(
      user.tenantId,
      campaignId,
      goal.platform as IntegrationPlatform,
      from,
      to,
      [goal.metricKey],
      MetricAggregate.SUM,
    );

    const actualValue = summary[goal.metricKey] ?? 0;
    const targetValue = Number(goal.targetValue);
    const progressPct = targetValue > 0 ? actualValue / targetValue : 0;
    const status = this.deriveStatus(progressPct);

    return {
      goal,
      actualValue,
      targetValue,
      progressPct: Math.round(progressPct * 10000) / 100, // e.g. 72.35
      status,
      daysRemaining: this.daysRemaining(goal.periodEnd),
    };
  }

  async getCampaignGoalsProgress(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
  ) {
    const goals = await this.list(user, clientId, campaignId);

    return Promise.all(
      goals.map(goal => this.getProgress(user, clientId, campaignId, goal.id)),
    );
  }

  // ─── Private helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  private async findGoal(tenantId: string, campaignId: string, goalId: string) {
    const goal = await this.prisma.goal.findFirst({
      where: { id: goalId, tenantId, campaignId, deletedAt: null },
    });
    if (!goal) throw new NotFoundException('Goal not found.');
    return goal;
  }

  private async assertCampaignAccess(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
  ) {
    const isClient = user.role === UserRole.CLIENT_USER;
    const campaign = await this.prisma.campaign.findFirst({
      where: {
        id: campaignId,
        clientId,
        tenantId: user.tenantId,
        deletedAt: null,
        ...(isClient && {
          client: { clientUserAssignments: { some: { userId: user.id } } },
        }),
      },
    });
    if (!campaign) throw new NotFoundException('Campaign not found.');
  }

  private assertAdmin(user: AuthenticatedUser) {
    if (!ADMIN_ROLES.includes(user.role as UserRole)) {
      throw new ForbiddenException('Only admins can manage goals.');
    }
  }

  private assertDateRange(start: string, end: string) {
    if (new Date(start) >= new Date(end)) {
      throw new BadRequestException('periodStart must be before periodEnd.');
    }
  }

  private deriveStatus(progressPct: number): string {
    if (progressPct >= 1) return 'ACHIEVED';
    if (progressPct >= ON_TRACK_THRESHOLD) return 'ON_TRACK';
    if (progressPct >= AT_RISK_THRESHOLD) return 'AT_RISK';
    return 'BEHIND';
  }

  private daysRemaining(periodEnd: Date): number {
    const diff = periodEnd.getTime() - Date.now();
    return Math.max(0, Math.ceil(diff / 86_400_000));
  }
}
Write goals.controller.ts
84 lines
import {
  Controller, Get, Post, Patch, Delete,
  Param, Body, HttpCode, HttpStatus,
} from '@nestjs/common';
import { ApiTags, ApiBearerAuth, ApiOperation } from '@nestjs/swagger';
import { GoalsService } from './goals.service';
import { CreateGoalDto } from './dto/create-goal.dto';
import { UpdateGoalDto } from './dto/update-goal.dto';
import { CurrentUser } from '../../common/decorators/current-user.decorator';
import { AuthenticatedUser } from '../auth/strategies/jwt.strategy';

@ApiTags('Goals')
@ApiBearerAuth()
@Controller('clients/:clientId/campaigns/:campaignId/goals')
export class GoalsController {
  constructor(private readonly goalsService: GoalsService) {}

  @Post()
  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Create a goal for a campaign' })
  create(
    @CurrentUser() user: AuthenticatedUser,
    @Param('clientId') clientId: string,
    @Param('campaignId') campaignId: string,
    @Body() dto: CreateGoalDto,
  ) {
    return this.goalsService.create(user, clientId, campaignId, dto);
  }

  @Get()
  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'List all goals for a campaign' })
  list(
    @CurrentUser() user: AuthenticatedUser,
    @Param('clientId') clientId: string,
    @Param('campaignId') campaignId: string,
  ) {
    return this.goalsService.list(user, clientId, campaignId);
  }

  @Get('progress')
  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Get progress for all goals in a campaign' })
  getCampaignProgress(
    @CurrentUser() user: AuthenticatedUser,
    @Param('clientId') clientId: string,
    @Param('campaignId') campaignId: string,
  ) {
    return this.goalsService.getCampaignGoalsProgress(user, clientId, campaignId);
  }

  @Get(':goalId/progress')
  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Get progress for a single goal' })
  getProgress(
    @CurrentUser() user: AuthenticatedUser,
    @Param('clientId') clientId: string,
    @Param('campaignId') campaignId: string,
    @Param('goalId') goalId: string,
  ) {
    return this.goalsService.getProgress(user, clientId, campaignId, goalId);
  }

  @Patch(':goalId')
  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Update a goal' })
  update(
    @CurrentUser() user: AuthenticatedUser,
    @Param('clientId') clientId: string,
    @Param('campaignId') campaignId: string,
    @Param('goalId') goalId: string,
    @Body() dto: UpdateGoalDto,
  ) {
    return this.goalsService.update(user, clientId, campaignId, goalId, dto);
  }

  @Delete(':goalId')
  @HttpCode(HttpStatus.NO_CONTENT)
  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Delete a goal (soft delete)' })
  remove(
    @CurrentUser() user: AuthenticatedUser,
    @Param('clientId') clientId: string,
    @Param('campaignId') campaignId: string,
    @Param('goalId') goalId: string,
  ) {
    return this.goalsService.remove(user, clientId, campaignId, goalId);
  }
}
Write goals.module.ts

import { Module } from '@nestjs/common';
import { GoalsController } from './goals.controller';
import { GoalsService } from './goals.service';
import { MetricsModule } from '../metrics/metrics.module';

@Module({

import { IsDateString, IsOptional, IsEnum, IsArray, IsString } from 'class-validator';
import { ApiProperty } from '@nestjs/swagger';
import { IntegrationPlatform } from '@prisma/client';
import { Transform } from 'class-transformer';

export class ScorecardQueryDto {
  @ApiProperty({ example: '2026-04-01' })
  @IsDateString()
  from: string;

  @ApiProperty({ example: '2026-04-30' })
  @IsDateString()
  to: string;

  @ApiProperty({ enum: IntegrationPlatform })
  @IsEnum(IntegrationPlatform)
  platform: IntegrationPlatform;

  @ApiProperty({ example: ['sessions', 'clicks'], required: false, type: [String] })
  @IsOptional()
  @IsArray()
  @IsString({ each: true })
  @Transform(({ value }) => (typeof value === 'string' ? value.split(',') : value))
  metricKeys?: string[];
}
Write scorecard.service.ts
136 lines
import { Injectable, NotFoundException } from '@nestjs/common';
import { UserRole, IntegrationPlatform } from '@prisma/client';
import { PrismaService } from '../../database/prisma.service';
import { MetricsService } from '../metrics/metrics.service';
import { MetricAggregate, MetricSummaryResult } from '../metrics/dto/query-metrics.dto';
import { AuthenticatedUser } from '../auth/strategies/jwt.strategy';
import { ScorecardQueryDto } from './dto/scorecard-query.dto';

// Change thresholds (relative to prior period)
const GOOD_THRESHOLD = -0.05;     // ≥ -5%  → GOOD
const WARNING_THRESHOLD = -0.15;  // -5% to -15% → WARNING
// worse than -15% → BAD

export interface ScorecardMetric {
  metricKey: string;
  currentValue: number;
  priorValue: number;
  changeAbsolute: number;
  changePct: number | null; // null when prior is 0
  status: 'GOOD' | 'WARNING' | 'BAD' | 'NEW'; // NEW = no prior data
}

export interface ScorecardResponse {
  campaignId: string;
  platform: IntegrationPlatform;
  currentPeriod: { from: string; to: string };
  priorPeriod: { from: string; to: string };
  metrics: ScorecardMetric[];
}

@Injectable()
export class ScorecardService {
  constructor(
    private readonly prisma: PrismaService,
    private readonly metrics: MetricsService,
  ) {}

  async getScorecard(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
    dto: ScorecardQueryDto,
  ): Promise<ScorecardResponse> {
    await this.assertCampaignAccess(user, clientId, campaignId);

    const priorPeriod = this.computePriorPeriod(dto.from, dto.to);

    const [current, prior] = await Promise.all([
      this.metrics.getMetricSummary(
        user.tenantId, campaignId, dto.platform,
        dto.from, dto.to, dto.metricKeys, MetricAggregate.SUM,
      ),
      this.metrics.getMetricSummary(
        user.tenantId, campaignId, dto.platform,
        priorPeriod.from, priorPeriod.to, dto.metricKeys, MetricAggregate.SUM,
      ),
    ]);

    const metricKeys = dto.metricKeys?.length
      ? dto.metricKeys
      : Object.keys(current);

    const scorecardMetrics: ScorecardMetric[] = metricKeys.map(key => {
      const currentValue = (current[key] as number) ?? 0;
      const priorValue = (prior[key] as number) ?? 0;
      const changeAbsolute = currentValue - priorValue;

      if (priorValue === 0) {
        return {
          metricKey: key,
          currentValue,
          priorValue,
          changeAbsolute,
          changePct: null,
          status: 'NEW' as const,
        };
      }

      const changePct = changeAbsolute / priorValue;
      const status = this.deriveStatus(changePct);

      return { metricKey: key, currentValue, priorValue, changeAbsolute, changePct, status };
    });

    return {
      campaignId,
      platform: dto.platform,
      currentPeriod: { from: dto.from, to: dto.to },
      priorPeriod,
      metrics: scorecardMetrics,
    };
  }

  // ─── Private helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  private computePriorPeriod(from: string, to: string): { from: string; to: string } {
    const start = new Date(from);
    const end = new Date(to);
    const durationMs = end.getTime() - start.getTime() + 86_400_000; // inclusive days

    const priorEnd = new Date(start.getTime() - 86_400_000);
    const priorStart = new Date(priorEnd.getTime() - durationMs + 86_400_000);

    return {
      from: priorStart.toISOString().slice(0, 10),
      to: priorEnd.toISOString().slice(0, 10),
    };
  }

  private deriveStatus(changePct: number): 'GOOD' | 'WARNING' | 'BAD' {
    if (changePct >= GOOD_THRESHOLD) return 'GOOD';
    if (changePct >= WARNING_THRESHOLD) return 'WARNING';
    return 'BAD';
  }

  private async assertCampaignAccess(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
  ) {
    const isClient = user.role === UserRole.CLIENT_USER;
    const campaign = await this.prisma.campaign.findFirst({
      where: {
        id: campaignId,
        clientId,
        tenantId: user.tenantId,
        deletedAt: null,
        ...(isClient && {
          client: { clientUserAssignments: { some: { userId: user.id } } },
        }),
      },
    });
    if (!campaign) throw new NotFoundException('Campaign not found.');
  }
}
Write scorecard.controller.ts

import { Controller, Get, Param, Query } from '@nestjs/common';
import { ApiTags, ApiBearerAuth, ApiOperation } from '@nestjs/swagger';
import { ScorecardService } from './scorecard.service';
import { ScorecardQueryDto } from './dto/scorecard-query.dto';
import { CurrentUser } from '../../common/decorators/current-user.decorator';
import { AuthenticatedUser } from '../auth/strategies/jwt.strategy';

@ApiTags('Scorecard')
@ApiBearerAuth()
@Controller('clients/:clientId/campaigns/:campaignId/scorecard')
export class ScorecardController {
  constructor(private readonly scorecardService: ScorecardService) {}

  @Get()
  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Get period-over-period scorecard for a campaign' })
  getScorecard(
    @CurrentUser() user: AuthenticatedUser,
    @Param('clientId') clientId: string,
    @Param('campaignId') campaignId: string,
    @Query() query: ScorecardQueryDto,
  ) {
    return this.scorecardService.getScorecard(user, clientId, campaignId, query);
  }
}

Write notes.service.ts
130 lines
import {
  Injectable,
  NotFoundException,
  ForbiddenException,
} from '@nestjs/common';
import { UserRole } from '@prisma/client';
import { PrismaService } from '../../database/prisma.service';
import { AuthenticatedUser } from '../auth/strategies/jwt.strategy';
import { CreateNoteDto } from './dto/create-note.dto';
import { UpdateNoteDto } from './dto/update-note.dto';

const ADMIN_ROLES: UserRole[] = [UserRole.AGENCY_OWNER, UserRole.AGENCY_ADMIN];

@Injectable()
export class NotesService {
  constructor(private readonly prisma: PrismaService) {}

  async create(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
    dto: CreateNoteDto,
  ) {
    await this.assertCampaignAccess(user, clientId, campaignId);

    return this.prisma.campaignNote.create({
      data: {
        tenantId: user.tenantId,
        campaignId,
        authorId: user.id,
        body: dto.body,
      },
      include: { author: { select: { id: true, firstName: true, lastName: true, role: true } } },
    } as any);
  }

  async list(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
  ) {
    await this.assertCampaignAccess(user, clientId, campaignId);

    return (this.prisma.campaignNote.findMany as any)({
      where: { tenantId: user.tenantId, campaignId, deletedAt: null },
      orderBy: [{ isPinned: 'desc' }, { createdAt: 'desc' }],
      include: { author: { select: { id: true, firstName: true, lastName: true, role: true } } },
    });
  }

  async update(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
    noteId: string,
    dto: UpdateNoteDto,
  ) {
    await this.assertCampaignAccess(user, clientId, campaignId);
    const note = await this.findNote(user.tenantId, campaignId, noteId);

    // Non-admins can only edit their own notes; isPinned is admin-only
    const isAdmin = ADMIN_ROLES.includes(user.role as UserRole);
    if (!isAdmin && note.authorId !== user.id) {
      throw new ForbiddenException('You can only edit your own notes.');
    }
    if (dto.isPinned !== undefined && !isAdmin) {
      throw new ForbiddenException('Only admins can pin notes.');
    }

    return (this.prisma.campaignNote.update as any)({
      where: { id: noteId },
      data: {
        ...(dto.body !== undefined && { body: dto.body }),
        ...(dto.isPinned !== undefined && { isPinned: dto.isPinned }),
      },
      include: { author: { select: { id: true, firstName: true, lastName: true, role: true } } },
    });
  }

  async remove(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
    noteId: string,
  ) {
    await this.assertCampaignAccess(user, clientId, campaignId);
    const note = await this.findNote(user.tenantId, campaignId, noteId);

    const isAdmin = ADMIN_ROLES.includes(user.role as UserRole);
    if (!isAdmin && note.authorId !== user.id) {
      throw new ForbiddenException('You can only delete your own notes.');
    }

    await (this.prisma.campaignNote.update as any)({
      where: { id: noteId },
      data: { deletedAt: new Date() },
    });
  }

  // ─── Private helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

  private async findNote(tenantId: string, campaignId: string, noteId: string) {
    const note = await (this.prisma.campaignNote.findFirst as any)({
      where: { id: noteId, tenantId, campaignId, deletedAt: null },
    });
    if (!note) throw new NotFoundException('Note not found.');
    return note;
  }

  private async assertCampaignAccess(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
  ) {
    const isClient = user.role === UserRole.CLIENT_USER;
    const campaign = await this.prisma.campaign.findFirst({
      where: {
        id: campaignId,
        clientId,
        tenantId: user.tenantId,
        deletedAt: null,
        ...(isClient && {
          client: { clientUserAssignments: { some: { userId: user.id } } },
        }),
      },
    });
    if (!campaign) throw new NotFoundException('Campaign not found.');
  }
}
Write notes.controller.ts
63 lines
import {
  Controller, Get, Post, Patch, Delete,
  Param, Body, HttpCode, HttpStatus,
} from '@nestjs/common';
import { ApiTags, ApiBearerAuth, ApiOperation } from '@nestjs/swagger';
import { NotesService } from './notes.service';
import { CreateNoteDto } from './dto/create-note.dto';
import { UpdateNoteDto } from './dto/update-note.dto';
import { CurrentUser } from '../../common/decorators/current-user.decorator';
import { AuthenticatedUser } from '../auth/strategies/jwt.strategy';

@ApiTags('Campaign Notes')
@ApiBearerAuth()
@Controller('clients/:clientId/campaigns/:campaignId/notes')
export class NotesController {
  constructor(private readonly notesService: NotesService) {}

  @Post()
  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Create a campaign note' })
  create(
    @CurrentUser() user: AuthenticatedUser,
    @Param('clientId') clientId: string,
    @Param('campaignId') campaignId: string,
    @Body() dto: CreateNoteDto,
  ) {
    return this.notesService.create(user, clientId, campaignId, dto);
  }

  @Get()
  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'List campaign notes (pinned first)' })
  list(
    @CurrentUser() user: AuthenticatedUser,
    @Param('clientId') clientId: string,
    @Param('campaignId') campaignId: string,
  ) {
    return this.notesService.list(user, clientId, campaignId);
  }

  @Patch(':noteId')
  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Update a note body or pin status' })
  update(
    @CurrentUser() user: AuthenticatedUser,
    @Param('clientId') clientId: string,
    @Param('campaignId') campaignId: string,
    @Param('noteId') noteId: string,
    @Body() dto: UpdateNoteDto,
  ) {
    return this.notesService.update(user, clientId, campaignId, noteId, dto);
  }

  @Delete(':noteId')
  @HttpCode(HttpStatus.NO_CONTENT)
  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Delete a note (soft delete)' })
  remove(
    @CurrentUser() user: AuthenticatedUser,
    @Param('clientId') clientId: string,
    @Param('campaignId') campaignId: string,
    @Param('noteId') noteId: string,
  ) {
    return this.notesService.remove(user, clientId, campaignId, noteId);
  }
}

export class NotesModule {}

Step 1: Create shared metrics-utils.ts

Write metrics-utils.ts
82 lines
/**
 * Shared utilities for period computation, change calculations, and status thresholds.
 * Used by scorecard, goals, and any future feature that compares metric periods.
 *
 * Time boundary convention (inclusive on both ends — matches MetricsService SQL):
 *   recorded_at >= $from::date AND recorded_at <= $to::date
 */

// ─── Period helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/**
 * Computes the immediately preceding period of the same duration.
 * E.g. Apr 1–30 (30 days) → Mar 2–31 (30 days prior).
 * Both input and output are YYYY-MM-DD strings.
 */
export function computePriorPeriod(from: string, to: string): { from: string; to: string } {
  const startMs = Date.UTC(
    parseInt(from.slice(0, 4), 10),
    parseInt(from.slice(5, 7), 10) - 1,
    parseInt(from.slice(8, 10), 10),
  );
  const endMs = Date.UTC(
    parseInt(to.slice(0, 4), 10),
    parseInt(to.slice(5, 7), 10) - 1,
    parseInt(to.slice(8, 10), 10),
  );

  // Duration in ms (inclusive: e.g. Apr 1–30 = 30 days = 30 * 86400000)
  const durationMs = endMs - startMs + 86_400_000;

  const priorEndMs = startMs - 86_400_000;         // day before current start
  const priorStartMs = priorEndMs - durationMs + 86_400_000; // same length prior

  return {
    from: msToDateString(priorStartMs),
    to: msToDateString(priorEndMs),
  };
}

function msToDateString(ms: number): string {
  return new Date(ms).toISOString().slice(0, 10);
}

// ─── Change calculations ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

/** Returns change as a decimal fraction (0.12 = +12%). Null when prior is 0. */
export function computeChangePct(current: number, prior: number): number | null {
  if (prior === 0) return null;
  return (current - prior) / prior;
}

// ─── Scorecard status thresholds ──────────────────────────────────────────────

// GOOD    ≥ -5%  (maintained or grew)
// WARNING  -5% to -15%  (slight decline)
// BAD     < -15%  (significant decline)

export type ScorecardStatus = 'GOOD' | 'WARNING' | 'BAD' | 'NEW';

export function scorecardStatus(changePct: number | null): ScorecardStatus {
  if (changePct === null) return 'NEW';
  if (changePct >= -0.05) return 'GOOD';
  if (changePct >= -0.15) return 'WARNING';
  return 'BAD';
}

// ─── Goal progress status thresholds ─────────────────────────────────────────

// ACHIEVED  ≥ 100%
// ON_TRACK  ≥  70%
// AT_RISK   ≥  40%
// BEHIND    <  40%

export type GoalStatus = 'ACHIEVED' | 'ON_TRACK' | 'AT_RISK' | 'BEHIND';

export function goalProgressStatus(progressFraction: number): GoalStatus {
  if (progressFraction >= 1) return 'ACHIEVED';
  if (progressFraction >= 0.7) return 'ON_TRACK';
  if (progressFraction >= 0.4) return 'AT_RISK';
  return 'BEHIND';
}
Step 2: Update scorecard service to use shared utils + add caching

Write scorecard.service.ts
111 lines
import { Injectable, NotFoundException } from '@nestjs/common';
import { UserRole, IntegrationPlatform } from '@prisma/client';
import { PrismaService } from '../../database/prisma.service';
import { CacheService } from '../../common/cache/cache.service';
import { MetricsService } from '../metrics/metrics.service';
import { MetricAggregate } from '../metrics/dto/query-metrics.dto';
import { AuthenticatedUser } from '../auth/strategies/jwt.strategy';
import {
  computePriorPeriod,
  computeChangePct,
  scorecardStatus,
  ScorecardStatus,
} from '../../common/metrics-utils';
import { ScorecardQueryDto } from './dto/scorecard-query.dto';

const SCORECARD_CACHE_TTL = 60; // 60 seconds

export interface ScorecardMetric {
  metricKey: string;
  currentValue: number;
  priorValue: number;
  changeAbsolute: number;
  changePct: number | null;
  status: ScorecardStatus;
}

export interface ScorecardResponse {
  campaignId: string;
  platform: IntegrationPlatform;
  currentPeriod: { from: string; to: string };
  priorPeriod: { from: string; to: string };
  metrics: ScorecardMetric[];
}

@Injectable()
export class ScorecardService {
  constructor(
    private readonly prisma: PrismaService,
    private readonly metrics: MetricsService,
    private readonly cache: CacheService,
  ) {}

  async getScorecard(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
    dto: ScorecardQueryDto,
  ): Promise<ScorecardResponse> {
    await this.assertCampaignAccess(user, clientId, campaignId);

    const priorPeriod = computePriorPeriod(dto.from, dto.to);
    const metricKeysStr = dto.metricKeys?.join(',') ?? 'all';
    const cacheKey = `scorecard:${user.tenantId}:${campaignId}:${dto.platform}:${dto.from}:${dto.to}:${metricKeysStr}`;

    return this.cache.getOrSet(cacheKey, SCORECARD_CACHE_TTL, async () => {
      const [current, prior] = await Promise.all([
        this.metrics.getMetricSummary(
          user.tenantId, campaignId, dto.platform,
          dto.from, dto.to, dto.metricKeys, MetricAggregate.SUM,
        ),
        this.metrics.getMetricSummary(
          user.tenantId, campaignId, dto.platform,
          priorPeriod.from, priorPeriod.to, dto.metricKeys, MetricAggregate.SUM,
        ),
      ]);

      const metricKeys = dto.metricKeys?.length
        ? dto.metricKeys
        : Object.keys(current.metrics);

      const scorecardMetrics: ScorecardMetric[] = metricKeys.map(key => {
        const currentValue = current.metrics[key] ?? 0;
        const priorValue = prior.metrics[key] ?? 0;
        const changeAbsolute = currentValue - priorValue;
        const changePct = computeChangePct(currentValue, priorValue);
        const status = scorecardStatus(changePct);

        return { metricKey: key, currentValue, priorValue, changeAbsolute, changePct, status };
      });

      return {
        campaignId,
        platform: dto.platform,
        currentPeriod: { from: dto.from, to: dto.to },
        priorPeriod,
        metrics: scorecardMetrics,
      };
    });
  }

  private async assertCampaignAccess(
    user: AuthenticatedUser,
    clientId: string,
    campaignId: string,
  ) {
    const isClient = user.role === UserRole.CLIENT_USER;
    const campaign = await this.prisma.campaign.findFirst({
      where: {
        id: campaignId,
        clientId,
        tenantId: user.tenantId,
        deletedAt: null,
        ...(isClient && {
          client: { clientUserAssignments: { some: { userId: user.id } } },
        }),
      },
    });
    if (!campaign) throw new NotFoundException('Campaign not found.');
  }
}

1. Consistent time boundaries — computePriorPeriod in common/metrics-utils.ts uses Date.UTC() (not new Date()) to avoid local timezone shifts. The convention is documented: inclusive start, inclusive end — matching MetricsService's SQL >=/$<=.

goalProgressStatus(progressFraction) — ACHIEVED/ON_TRACK/AT_RISK/BEHIND thresholds in one place
3. Caching added:

Health (campaign) → 30s cache keyed by tenantId:campaignId
Health (agency) → 30s cache keyed by tenantId:(all|userId) — client users get their own cache slot
Scorecard → 60s cache keyed by all query params
Goal progress → 60s cache keyed by tenantId:goalId:from:to
4. Rate limiting:

GET /agencies/health → 20 req/min
GET /scorecard → 30 req/min

The user wants a detailed plan for the next 4 Phase 8 features:

8.1 — Alerts & Monitoring
8.8 — ROI Forecasting
8.3 — KPI Engine
8.7 — Data Export
They want me to plan only, not implement. Let me think through each one carefully, considering what AgencyAnalytics and similar platforms do, and design production-ready, industry-standard solutions.

Let me think through each feature deeply:

8.1 — Alerts & Monitoring
What AgencyAnalytics does:

Threshold alerts: if metric drops below/above a value, send email

Budget alerts: when ad spend reaches X% of budget

Anomaly alerts (more advanced): statistical deviation from baseline

Alert digest: daily/weekly summary vs individual fire-per-breach

Production-ready design:
I'll need an alerts table storing the configuration—tenantId, campaignId, platform, metricKey, condition type, threshold, frequency, and recipient list—plus an alert_events table for the audit trail. A BullMQ job runs post-sync to evaluate all active alerts, and I'll implement deduplication so the same alert doesn't fire multiple times within a window.

The alert conditions support simple thresholds, percentage changes versus prior periods, and budget pacing checks. After each campaign sync, I'll enqueue an alert check job that evaluates the latest metrics against all active rules. Notifications include context like current values and trends.

The API exposes standard CRUD endpoints for managing alerts and a history endpoint to view past trigger events.

Now I'm moving into ROI forecasting—looking at what competitors typically implement, which is usually just linear trend extrapolation.

For the production implementation, I'll compute forecasts on-demand from existing metric data without needing new tables. The endpoint accepts parameters for platform, metric, date range, and forecast horizon. I'm using linear regression on the last N days of data to project forward with confidence bands, requiring at least 7 days of history to generate a forecast. The results cache for an hour since forecasts are relatively stable, and the response includes both historical actuals and projected values with upper and lower bounds.

The regression calculation uses standard least squares formulas to find the slope and intercept, then computes R² to measure how well the line fits the data. The confidence band extends ±1 standard deviation from the regression line based on residual variance.

Now I'm moving into custom calculated metrics — AgencyAnalytics lets users define derived KPIs like ROAS or CPA through formula builders, combining raw metrics with arithmetic operations.

For the implementation, I need two layers: built-in formulas for standard metrics like CTR, CPC, CPM, ROAS, CPA, and conversion rate, plus a custom metric system where users can write their own expressions. The custom metrics store the formula as a string and track which base metrics they depend on, then evaluate them safely using a sandboxed expression parser rather than eval. The API endpoint fetches campaign KPIs across a date range and platform.

For custom formulas, I'm restricting the syntax to basic math operators and variables in $metricKey format, capping formula length at 500 characters, and always evaluating server-side to prevent SQL injection. Then for data export, I'm starting with CSV and Excel downloads that let users pull campaign metrics across a time period with specific metric keys, and planning Google Sheets integration as a later phase.

For the export endpoints, I'm streaming CSV responses directly without storing files, using the exceljs library for Excel generation, and implementing strict rate limiting at 10 requests per minute since these are expensive operations. The service will cap large date ranges at 365 days, apply the same role-based access controls as the metrics endpoints, and generate filenames that include the campaign name, platform, and date range for clarity.

Let me read the current state of key files before planning to make sure the plan is grounded in what's actually built.

import { Module } from '@nestjs/common';
import { BullModule } from '@nestjs/bullmq';
import { DatabaseModule } from '../../database/database.module';
import { IntegrationsModule } from '../integrations/integrations.module';
import { Ga4Module } from '../integrations/ga4/ga4.module';
import { GoogleAdsModule } from '../integrations/google-ads/google-ads.module';
import { MetaAdsModule } from '../integrations/meta-ads/meta-ads.module';
import { MetricsModule } from '../metrics/metrics.module';
import { SYNC_QUEUE } from './constants/sync-queue.constants';
import { IntegrationSyncProcessor } from './processors/integration-sync.processor';
import { SyncService } from './sync.service';
import { SyncSchedulerService } from './sync-scheduler.service';
import { SyncController } from './sync.controller';

@Module({
  imports: [
    BullModule.registerQueue({
      name: SYNC_QUEUE,
      defaultJobOptions: {
        attempts: 3,
        backoff: { type: 'custom' }, // strategy defined in IntegrationSyncProcessor settings
        removeOnComplete: { count: 100 },        // keep last 100 completed jobs
        removeOnFail: { age: 7 * 24 * 3600 },    // keep failed jobs for 7 days (debugging)
      },
    }),

    // PrismaService — campaign validation in controller + connection queries in processor
    DatabaseModule,

    // IntegrationsModule — markSynced / markExpired / markError / getDecryptedTokens
    IntegrationsModule,

    // Platform modules — OAuth services (getValidAccessToken) + API services (fetch data)
    Ga4Module,
    GoogleAdsModule,
    MetaAdsModule,

    // MetricsModule — upsertMetrics called by processors after fetching data
    MetricsModule,
  ],
  controllers: [SyncController],
  providers: [SyncService, SyncSchedulerService, IntegrationSyncProcessor],
  // Export SyncService so Phase 3.6 (cron scheduler) can dispatch jobs
  exports: [SyncService],
})
export class SyncModule {}

import { IntegrationPlatform } from '@prisma/client';

/**
 * Seed data for metric_definitions — defines what metrics each platform produces.
 * Used by dashboards to know what's available and how to display it.
 *
 * category: traffic | engagement | cost | conversion
 * dataType: integer | decimal | percentage | currency
 */
export const METRIC_SEEDS: Array<{
  platform: IntegrationPlatform;
  metricKey: string;
  label: string;
  category: string;
  dataType: string;
  unit: string | null;
}> = [
  // ─── GA4 ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  { platform: 'GA4', metricKey: 'sessions',                label: 'Sessions',                 category: 'traffic',    dataType: 'integer',    unit: 'count' },
  { platform: 'GA4', metricKey: 'totalUsers',              label: 'Total Users',              category: 'traffic',    dataType: 'integer',    unit: 'count' },
  { platform: 'GA4', metricKey: 'newUsers',                label: 'New Users',                category: 'traffic',    dataType: 'integer',    unit: 'count' },
  { platform: 'GA4', metricKey: 'screenPageViews',         label: 'Page Views',               category: 'traffic',    dataType: 'integer',    unit: 'count' },
  { platform: 'GA4', metricKey: 'bounceRate',              label: 'Bounce Rate',              category: 'engagement', dataType: 'percentage', unit: '%' },
  { platform: 'GA4', metricKey: 'averageSessionDuration',  label: 'Avg Session Duration',     category: 'engagement', dataType: 'decimal',    unit: 'seconds' },

  // ─── Google Ads (values normalized before storage: micros → USD, ctr → %) ──
  { platform: 'GOOGLE_ADS', metricKey: 'clicks',       label: 'Clicks',        category: 'traffic',    dataType: 'integer',    unit: 'count' },
  { platform: 'GOOGLE_ADS', metricKey: 'impressions',   label: 'Impressions',   category: 'traffic',    dataType: 'integer',    unit: 'count' },
  { platform: 'GOOGLE_ADS', metricKey: 'ctr',           label: 'CTR',           category: 'engagement', dataType: 'percentage', unit: '%' },
  { platform: 'GOOGLE_ADS', metricKey: 'avg_cpc',       label: 'Avg CPC',       category: 'cost',       dataType: 'currency',   unit: 'USD' },

Phase 8.1 / 8.8 / 8.3 / 8.7 — Full Implementation Plan
8.1 — Alerts & Monitoring
What AgencyAnalytics does
Threshold alerts that fire when a metric crosses a value, budget pacing alerts when spend approaches a cap, and anomaly detection. Alerts notify via email with context (current value, threshold, trend direction). Alert history is preserved.

DB Schema — 2 new tables
alerts


id, tenant_id, campaign_id, platform, metric_key,
condition      ENUM(ABOVE, BELOW, PERCENT_CHANGE_ABOVE, PERCENT_CHANGE_BELOW),
threshold      DECIMAL(20,6),          -- the target number
period_type    ENUM(DAILY, WEEKLY),    -- evaluate over what window
recipient_emails TEXT[],
is_active      BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
cooldown_hours  INTEGER DEFAULT 24,    -- don't re-fire within this window
last_triggered_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
created_by_id, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
alert_events (immutable audit log — no soft delete)


id, tenant_id, alert_id, campaign_id,
triggered_value  DECIMAL(20,6),   -- value that caused the trigger
threshold_value  DECIMAL(20,6),   -- snapshot of threshold at fire time
condition        TEXT,
notified_at      TIMESTAMPTZ,
emails_sent      TEXT[],
created_at
BullMQ: ALERT_CHECK_QUEUE
New queue alongside SYNC_QUEUE and REPORT_QUEUE.

Trigger point: after every successful upsertMetrics() in IntegrationSyncProcessor, enqueue an AlertCheckJob with { tenantId, campaignId, platform }. This is fire-and-forget — sync should not fail if alert check fails.

AlertCheckProcessor:

Fetch all active, non-deleted alerts for (tenantId, campaignId, platform)
For each alert, skip if lastTriggeredAt is within cooldownHours
Fetch getMetricSummary for today (DAILY) or last 7 days (WEEKLY)
For PERCENT_CHANGE_*: also fetch prior same-length period using computePriorPeriod
Evaluate condition — if triggered:
Insert alert_event record
Update alerts.last_triggered_at = NOW()
Send email via EmailService.sendAlertTriggered()
Concurrency: 5 (cheap DB reads, multiple tenants)
jobId = alert:${alertId}:${todayDateStr} — BullMQ dedup so if sync runs twice today, alert only checks once
Email template: alert-triggered.hbs
Campaign name, platform, metric label, current value, threshold, condition text, link to campaign
API Endpoints

POST   /clients/:clientId/campaigns/:campaignId/alerts       -- ADMIN only
GET    /clients/:clientId/campaigns/:campaignId/alerts       -- all roles (scoped)
PATCH  /clients/:clientId/campaigns/:campaignId/alerts/:id   -- ADMIN only
DELETE /clients/:clientId/campaigns/:campaignId/alerts/:id   -- ADMIN only (soft delete)
GET    /clients/:clientId/campaigns/:campaignId/alerts/:id/events  -- history
Condition evaluation logic
Condition	Trigger when
ABOVE	currentValue > threshold
BELOW	currentValue < threshold
PERCENT_CHANGE_ABOVE	(current - prior) / prior * 100 > threshold
PERCENT_CHANGE_BELOW	(current - prior) / prior * 100 < threshold (negative threshold = drop alert)
Module structure

src/modules/alerts/
  dto/create-alert.dto.ts
  alerts.controller.ts
  alerts.service.ts
  alerts.module.ts
  processors/alert-check.processor.ts
  templates/alert-triggered.hbs   (in email module)
8.8 — ROI Forecasting
What AgencyAnalytics does
A projected trend line on time-series charts based on historical data. Shows where the metric is heading. Optionally shows a confidence band. Used for client presentations — "at current pace, you'll hit X by end of month."

No new DB tables — pure computation on metric_values
Algorithm: Ordinary Least Squares Linear Regression
Pull last N days of daily values (default: lookback window = same as the request from→to range, max 90 days)
Assign x = 0, 1, 2, ... n-1 for each day
Compute slope and intercept
Project forward forecastDays (default 30, max 90)
Compute R² (goodness of fit — send to frontend so it knows if trend is reliable)
Confidence band: ±1.96 × std deviation of residuals (95% confidence interval)
Minimum data: at least 7 data points — return { insufficient_data: true } if less
Response shape

{
  "metricKey": "sessions",
  "platform": "GA4",
  "trend": {
    "slope": 12.5,
    "intercept": 830,
    "r2": 0.87,
    "direction": "UP" | "DOWN" | "FLAT"
  },
  "historical": [
    { "date": "2026-04-01", "actual": 850 },
    ...
  ],
  "forecast": [
    { "date": "2026-05-01", "projected": 1230, "lower": 1100, "upper": 1360 },
    ...
  ],
  "forecastSummary": {
    "projectedTotal": 38400,
    "projectedEndValue": 1450,
    "confidenceLevel": 0.95
  }
}
direction: FLAT if |slope / mean| < 0.02 (2%), otherwise UP/DOWN.

API Endpoint

GET /clients/:clientId/campaigns/:campaignId/forecast
    ?platform=GA4
    &metricKey=sessions
    &from=2026-04-01         (historical window start)
    &to=2026-04-30           (historical window end)
    &forecastDays=30         (default 30, max 90)
Caching
1-hour TTL (key: forecast:tenantId:campaignId:platform:metricKey:from:to:forecastDays)
Historical data is immutable once synced; forecasts don't need to refresh often
Rate limiting
@Throttle({ default: { limit: 20, ttl: 60_000 } }) — computation is cheap (in-memory math after one DB read), but still throttle
Module structure

src/modules/forecast/
  dto/forecast-query.dto.ts
  forecast.controller.ts
  forecast.service.ts          -- calls MetricsService.getMetrics(), runs OLS
  forecast.module.ts           -- imports MetricsModule, CacheModule
  utils/linear-regression.ts   -- pure math, unit-testable
8.3 — KPI Engine / Custom Calculated Metrics
What AgencyAnalytics does
Derived metrics computed from base metrics: ROAS, CPA, CPM, Conversion Rate, custom formulas. These appear as first-class metrics in dashboards, reports, and scorecards. Custom metrics are tenant-defined (not platform-level).

Two-layer design
Layer 1 — Built-in derived metrics (hardcoded, no DB)
Standard industry formulas that always exist for platforms that have the base metrics:

KPI	Formula	Platforms
ctr	clicks / impressions * 100	Google Ads, Meta Ads (already stored raw — recalculate cleanly)
cpc	spend_or_cost / clicks	Google Ads, Meta Ads
cpm	spend_or_cost / impressions * 1000	Google Ads, Meta Ads
roas	revenue / spend	Meta Ads (if revenue tracked)
cpa	spend_or_cost / conversions	Google Ads, Meta Ads
conversion_rate	conversions / clicks * 100	Google Ads, Meta Ads
pages_per_session	screenPageViews / sessions	GA4
These are computed in KpiEngineService.computeDerived(summaryResult, platform) — receives a MetricSummaryResult, returns it augmented with derived values. Zero-safe (if denominator = 0, result = null).

Layer 2 — Custom metric definitions (DB-persisted, tenant-scoped)

New table: custom_metric_definitions


id, tenant_id, name VARCHAR(100), 
formula TEXT,               -- e.g. "revenue / spend"
variable_keys TEXT[],       -- ["revenue", "spend"] — extracted at save time
platform integration_platform,
is_active BOOLEAN DEFAULT true,
created_by_id, created_at, updated_at, deleted_at
Formula language: only +, -, *, /, (, ) and $variableName references. Validated server-side with a whitelist regex parser (no eval, no SQL, no arbitrary code).

Formula evaluator (safe, no eval)
Parse with simple tokenizer: numbers, operators, parentheses, variable refs
Build an AST, evaluate with substituted values
Library: expr-eval (npm, 1.2kB, no eval, supports standard math)
KPI Engine endpoint

GET /clients/:clientId/campaigns/:campaignId/kpi
    ?platform=GA4
    &from=2026-04-01
    &to=2026-04-30
Returns: { base: { sessions: 12000, ... }, derived: { pages_per_session: 3.2, ... }, custom: { my_roi: 4.1, ... } }

Custom metric CRUD

POST   /agencies/me/kpi-definitions        -- ADMIN only
GET    /agencies/me/kpi-definitions        -- all agency users
PATCH  /agencies/me/kpi-definitions/:id   -- ADMIN only
DELETE /agencies/me/kpi-definitions/:id   -- ADMIN only (soft delete)
Module structure

src/modules/kpi/
  dto/create-kpi-definition.dto.ts
  dto/kpi-query.dto.ts
  kpi.controller.ts        -- GET /kpi, CRUD for /kpi-definitions
  kpi.service.ts           -- computeDerived() + evaluateCustom()
  kpi.module.ts            -- imports MetricsModule, CacheModule
  utils/formula-evaluator.ts   -- safe expression parser using expr-eval
  constants/built-in-kpis.ts   -- hardcoded formulas
8.7 — Data Export
What AgencyAnalytics does
CSV and Excel export of campaign metric data. Users download it for their own analysis, client presentations, or feeding into other tools. Format includes a header row, one row per day, metrics as columns.

Formats supported
CSV: streamed, Content-Type: text/csv, Content-Disposition: attachment
Excel (.xlsx): ExcelJS library (lightweight, no native dependencies, streaming support)
Google Sheets push: Phase 8.7b, later (OAuth to Google Sheets API — separate from GA4 OAuth)
API Endpoints

GET /clients/:clientId/campaigns/:campaignId/export
    ?platform=GA4
    &from=2026-04-01
    &to=2026-04-30
    &format=csv|xlsx          (default: csv)
    &metricKeys=sessions,totalUsers   (optional — omit for all)
    &granularity=day|week|month       (default: day)

GET /clients/:clientId/campaigns/:campaignId/export/summary
    ?platform=GA4&from=...&to=...&format=csv|xlsx
    (single-row KPI totals, not time-series)
Response
CSV: Content-Type: text/csv, Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="Campaign-Name_GA4_2026-04-01_2026-04-30.csv"
XLSX: Content-Type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet
CSV format (time-series)

Date,Sessions,Total Users,Page Views,...
2026-04-01,1200,980,3400,...
2026-04-02,1350,1100,3900,...
Excel format
Sheet 1: "Data" — same as CSV
Sheet 2: "Summary" — KPI totals, min, max, avg
Styled header row (bold, colored background matching agency primaryColor if available)
Auto-column width
Guards
Date range cap: max 365 days for day granularity, max 5 years for month granularity
Rate limiting: @Throttle({ default: { limit: 10, ttl: 60_000 } }) — expensive memory operation
Role scoping: same rules as metrics endpoint (CLIENT_USER restricted to assigned campaigns)
No file storage: generate entirely in memory, stream directly — no MinIO, no cleanup jobs
Module structure

src/modules/export/
  dto/export-query.dto.ts
  export.controller.ts
  export.service.ts      -- calls MetricsService, formats CSV / XLSX
  export.module.ts       -- imports MetricsModule
  utils/csv-formatter.ts
  utils/xlsx-formatter.ts
Dependency
exceljs — adds to package.json. Pure JS, no native bindings, works on Windows + Linux. ~2MB.

Cross-Cutting: What Makes This Production-Ready
Concern	Solution
Alert double-fire	cooldownHours + BullMQ jobId dedup per alertId:date
Alert on stale data	Only enqueued post-sync, never on raw API call
Forecast on sparse data	Minimum 7 points guard + insufficient_data flag + R² exposed
KPI formula injection	expr-eval whitelist parser, no eval, variable refs only
Export memory safety	365-day cap + streamed response (no temp file), @Throttle
Caching	Alerts list 30s, forecast 1h, KPI 5min (shares MetricsService TTL), export not cached (always fresh)
Role scoping	All endpoints follow CLIENT_USER assignment check pattern (same as metrics)
Audit trail	alert_events immutable table; custom_metric_definitions has created_by_id

Read d:\projects\agency-backend\improvements.txt
The improvements.txt now has a new review specifically for the 8.1/8.8/8.3/8.7 plan. Let me analyze each point and implement them:

7 CRITICAL fixes:

Alert jobId design fix:

Wrong: alert:${alertId}:${todayDateStr}
Right: alert:${alertId}:${periodType}:${dateStr} — because weekly alerts have a different date key than daily
Also: the queue explosion fix at the bottom says the check job should be: alert-check:{tenantId}:{campaignId}:{platform}:{date} (one job per campaign/platform/day, not per alert)
PERCENT_CHANGE divide-by-zero: Need to handle the case where prior is zero by either skipping the calculation or treating it as a new value

Alert spam: Consolidate all triggered alerts for a campaign into a single batch email instead of sending one per alert

Forecast outlier handling: Expose low R² values (below 0.3) as confidence warnings to the user

KPI variable validation: Verify at creation time that all variable references in the KPI definition actually exist in the platform's metric definitions

Export memory risk: Switch to streaming CSV output row-by-row for large datasets to avoid loading everything into memory

Alert evaluation N+1: Looking at the remaining optimization...

For the alert N+1 issue, I should group alerts by platform and period, fetch the metric summary once per group, then evaluate all alerts in that group against that single result. I also want to add a severity enum to alerts, set up proper KPI caching with the tenant, campaign, platform, and date range in the key, sanitize export filenames, and keep the forecast direction threshold logic as planned. I'm also clarifying the job ID structure—the alert check job uses one key per sync completion while individual alert evaluations use a different dedup key.

Now I'm ready to implement. The architecture is: after sync completes, enqueue one alert-check job per tenant-campaign-platform-date combination to prevent queue explosion, then the processor fetches all alerts for that campaign and platform, groups them, evaluates them together, and batches the emails. I'll start with the database migrations for the alerts, alert events, and custom metric definitions tables, then update the Prisma schema, build out the AlertsService and controller with the severity field and cooldown logic, implement the AlertCheckProcessor with the N+1 fix and batch email handling, create the email template, and wire it into the SyncProcessor as a fire-and-forget job with proper deduplication.

Implementing the remaining services next...

Actually, looking back at the review feedback more carefully: the alert jobId needs to be scoped per-alert and period type with the date, so alert:${alertId}:${periodType}:${dateStr}, and I need to handle the queue explosion risk by batching the check processor to run once per campaign+platform+day instead of per-alert. The AlertCheckProcessor will fetch all active alerts for a given tenant, campaign, and platform, group them by period type, and pull a single metric summary for each group. It'll evaluate each alert while skipping those in cooldown, collect the triggered ones, batch them into a single email per campaign, update the last triggered timestamp for each alert, and log the events. Now I need to implement all four features systematically, starting with installing exceljs for Excel export and expr-eval for evaluating KPI formulas—though I should first verify if these are already in the package.json.

All 7 critical fixes are valid and important. Let me implement them all as part of the full 8.1/8.8/8.3/8.7 implementation, with every fix baked in from the start.

-- CreateEnum
CREATE TYPE "alert_condition" AS ENUM (
  'ABOVE',
  'BELOW',
  'PERCENT_CHANGE_ABOVE',
  'PERCENT_CHANGE_BELOW'
);

-- CreateEnum
CREATE TYPE "alert_period_type" AS ENUM ('DAILY', 'WEEKLY');

-- CreateEnum
CREATE TYPE "alert_severity" AS ENUM ('INFO', 'WARNING', 'CRITICAL');

-- CreateTable
CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
    "id" UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    "tenant_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "campaign_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "name" VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    "platform" "integration_platform" NOT NULL,
    "metric_key" VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    "condition" "alert_condition" NOT NULL,
    "threshold" DECIMAL(20,6) NOT NULL,
    "period_type" "alert_period_type" NOT NULL DEFAULT 'DAILY',
    "severity" "alert_severity" NOT NULL DEFAULT 'WARNING',
    "recipient_emails" TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT ARRAY[]::TEXT[],
    "cooldown_hours" INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 24,
    "is_active" BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
    "last_triggered_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6),
    "created_by_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "created_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "updated_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "deleted_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6),

    CONSTRAINT "alerts_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);

-- CreateTable: immutable audit log — no soft delete, no updated_at
CREATE TABLE "alert_events" (
    "id" UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    "tenant_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "alert_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "campaign_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "triggered_value" DECIMAL(20,6) NOT NULL,
    "threshold_value" DECIMAL(20,6) NOT NULL,
    "condition" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "severity" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "emails_sent" TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT ARRAY[]::TEXT[],
    "notified_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,

    CONSTRAINT "alert_events_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);

-- CreateTable
CREATE TABLE "custom_metric_definitions" (
    "id" UUID NOT NULL DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    "tenant_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "name" VARCHAR(100) NOT NULL,
    "formula" TEXT NOT NULL,
    "variable_keys" TEXT[] NOT NULL DEFAULT ARRAY[]::TEXT[],
    "platform" "integration_platform" NOT NULL,
    "is_active" BOOLEAN NOT NULL DEFAULT true,
    "created_by_id" UUID NOT NULL,
    "created_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "updated_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6) NOT NULL DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
    "deleted_at" TIMESTAMPTZ(6),

    CONSTRAINT "custom_metric_definitions_pkey" PRIMARY KEY ("id")
);

-- CreateIndex
CREATE INDEX "idx_alerts_campaign" ON "alerts"("tenant_id", "campaign_id", "deleted_at");
CREATE INDEX "idx_alerts_active" ON "alerts"("tenant_id", "campaign_id", "platform", "is_active") WHERE "deleted_at" IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX "idx_alert_events_alert" ON "alert_events"("tenant_id", "alert_id");
CREATE INDEX "idx_alert_events_campaign" ON "alert_events"("tenant_id", "campaign_id", "notified_at" DESC);
CREATE INDEX "idx_custom_metrics_tenant" ON "custom_metric_definitions"("tenant_id", "platform", "deleted_at");
